title: Node Fetch, Redirect Control, Streams, Timeouts, and Bounded Bodies · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded Fetch response adapter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Set honest User-Agent, Accept, conditional validators, safe methods, credentials policy, and manual redirect handling through one adapter.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, encoding, validator, and domain outcome before parsing.
Count compressed, decoded, consumed, and retained bytes during stream processing and cancel over-budget bodies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Fetch Request Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 2533 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove honest Fetch requests, response admission, streaming byte limits, composed timeouts, and cleanup; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set honest User-Agent, Accept, conditional validators, safe methods, credentials policy, and manual redirect handling through one adapter.

Inspect the Fetch Request Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Body Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-body-cleanup-srrwrb" that uses case 7771 to prove honest Fetch requests, response admission, streaming byte limits, composed timeouts, and cleanup. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Body Cleanup.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Body Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Body Cleanup, keep Body Cleanup connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
